templateMeta.name: Commit Summary Telegram
templateMeta.description: Extract interesting features and updates from repository commits and post highlights to Telegram

timePeriod.description: Time period for commits (e.g., '7 days', '1 week', '24 hours')

fields.name: messageBody
fields.description: The actual message content that was posted

invariants.condition: You fetch commits from {{repoUrl}} for the time period {{timePeriod}} (relative to {{currentTimestamp}}) using the list_commits tool
invariants.assessment: Verify list_commits was called with correct owner/repo parsed from {{repoUrl}}, and since/until parameters calculated as explicit timestamps. For example, if timePeriod is '10 minutes' and currentTimestamp is '2026-01-30T16:42:00Z', then since should be '2026-01-30T16:32:00Z' and until should be '2026-01-30T16:42:00Z'.

do: Calculate explicit timestamps: timePeriod '10 minutes' at 16:42 → since: 16:32, until: 16:42
Use per_page: 100 to get full history
dont: Guess commit history without calling list_commits
Forget to set the since parameter for date filtering
Use vague time references instead of explicit ISO timestamps

invariants.condition: You extract the MOST INTERESTING features, improvements, and updates - NOT a raw commit dump
invariants.assessment: Message highlights concrete outcomes (new features, bug fixes, improvements) in user-friendly language. Groups related commits into logical changes.

do: Group related commits: 'Added dark mode support' (from 5 related commits)
Highlight user impact: 'Fixed critical login bug affecting mobile users'
Use emojis for visual appeal: '🚀 New feature: API rate limiting'
dont: List raw commit messages: 'fix: update index.ts'
Include trivial commits: 'refactor: clean up code'

invariants.condition: You post the highlights to Telegram using telegram_send_message, including a link back to the workstream on explorer
invariants.assessment: telegram_send_message was called. Message includes: project name, time period summary, key highlights formatted for readability, and a footer link to the workstream on explorer (use JINN_CTX_REQUEST_ID env var for the workstream ID)

do: Structure with clear sections: header, highlights, footer
Read JINN_CTX_REQUEST_ID from environment and include footer: '🔗 View workstream'
dont: Forget the workstream link in the footer

invariants.condition: Your final artifact includes: messageLink, messageId, messageBody, and commitCount
invariants.assessment: create_artifact was called with topic 'COMMIT_SUMMARY_RESULT' containing all four output fields extracted from telegram_send_message response

do: Include the full messageBody that was posted
Count total commits analyzed for commitCount
dont: Return without creating the result artifact
